senses.definition: A term with no widely recognized meaning in standard English; may be a personal or fictional name or a misspelling of another word.
examples: The manuscript includes a reference to shangalla that scholars debate.
In the fantasy world, a token called shangalla unlocked the gate.

senses.definition: No common meaning in contemporary English; possibly a proper noun or a transliteration from another language.
examples: The map marks Shangan as a small district.
Researchers compared Shangan with neighboring regions.

senses.definition: To force someone to go to a place (often aboard a ship) without consent, typically by kidnapping or coercion.
examples: They tried to shanghai the crew into leaving early.
Forty sailors were shanghaied into service during the storm.

senses.definition: The act of shanghaiing; coercive kidnapping or conscription.
examples: The ship's shanghai was infamous among mariners.
Stories describe brutal shanghaiings practiced in old ports.

senses.definition: Past tense of shanghai; coerced or forced someone into service, often at sea.
examples: The sailors were shanghaied into duty during the voyage.
Many workers were shanghaied to the ship against their will.

senses.definition: A person who shanghaies others; a kidnapper or coercer of sailors.
examples: The ship's captain was notorious as a ruthless shanghaier.
Old legends name him as a feared shanghaier in port towns.

senses.definition: Plural of shanghaier; multiple people who shanghai others.
examples: The portyards were watched by several notorious shanghaiers.
Histories mention many shanghaiers operating in the 19th century.

senses.definition: The act of shanghaiing someone; forced conscription or kidnapping.
examples: Shanghaiing was common on some old trading ships.
Accounts describe the brutal practice of shanghaiing.

senses.definition: A piece of the shank bone or a bent portion of meat; a term used in butchery or cooking.
examples: The recipe used the shankpiece for a rich stew.
Trim the shankpiece and braise slowly.

senses.definition: Plural form of shankpiece; multiple pieces of shank bone or meat.
examples: The butcher cut several shankpieces for the crowd.
Shankpieces are ideal for slow-cooked dishes.

senses.definition: Cylindrical parts of an animal leg or bone between the knee and ankle; can also refer to the part of a tool or meat cut.
examples: She ordered a roast shank with vegetables.
The turkey shanks were nicely browned.

senses.definition: A male worker or man associated with shanks in historical contexts; not common in modern usage.
examples: The old shipyard employed a rough shanksman crowd.
Stories describe a shanksman as a port labourer.

senses.definition: A given name; no inherent meaning beyond the person it refers to.
examples: Shanna wrote a letter to the editor.
Shanna’s artwork was displayed at the gallery.

senses.definition: Plural of Shannie; a given name or nickname as a plural reference.
examples: The siblings were connected through their Shannies.
Shannies joined the community group.

senses.definition: A surname or given name; also a common given name in English-speaking countries.
examples: Shannon is joining the group later today.
The Shannon family owns a small cafe.

senses.definition: A slang or affectionate diminutive form of Shannon; can also refer to a young eel in some dialects, but primarily a nickname.
examples: Shanny invited us to her birthday party.
The old sailors called him Shanny.
